name: claudecog
description: Read any codebase like a senior engineer would. Maps the architecture in 3D, walks you through any file, and points out the risks that actually bite.
long_description:
A repo is rarely a system to anyone but the people who built it. ClaudeCog gives that view back to you.

Point Claude at any folder on your machine and ask:

- **Map this folder.** Modules, what each one does, how they connect, walked through in 3D.
- **Explain a file.** Like having a senior engineer next to you for ten minutes. No syntax recap, only intent.
- **Find the real risks.** Security gaps, reliability hazards, silent coupling, dependency rot. No lint nits.

## How you pay for Claude

Claude Desktop does not let extensions use your Pro/Team subscription directly (the MCP 'sampling' feature is not implemented yet, see anthropics/claude-code#1785). So every extension that calls Claude needs its own access. Two options:

**Free, if you have Claude Pro/Max already.** Open a terminal and run these three commands once:

```
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
claude login
```

Then leave the API key field below empty. ClaudeCog detects the local `claude` CLI automatically and rides on your existing subscription. Zero extra cost.

**Paid, takes 30 seconds.** Get an API key at console.anthropic.com/settings/keys, add 5 USD of credit, paste the key in the field below. A typical analysis costs cents. The credit lasts months.

## About the warning above

Claude Desktop shows it for any extension that did not go through Anthropic's review. ClaudeCog only reads the folders you ask it to, never writes to your disk, and only sends short file summaries back to your own Anthropic account. The full source is open at github.com/felipepreseti/claudecog if you want to read it before installing.

tools.name: cog_map
tools.description: Map a folder as a system. Returns the modules, how they relate, a one-paragraph summary, and a link to the 3D walkthrough.

tools.name: cog_explain
tools.description: Walk through a single file like a senior engineer pairing with you for ten minutes.

tools.name: cog_risks
tools.description: Surface the risks that will actually hurt this team in production, in 6 months, or during onboarding. No lint nits.

api_key.title: Anthropic API key (optional if Claude Code is installed)
api_key.description: Paste a key from console.anthropic.com/settings/keys. Or leave empty if you've installed the Claude Code CLI to ride on your Claude Pro subscription for free.
